by_judge.judge: P SREE SUDHA IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 23.06.2026

narrative:
On June 23, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 47.6%, addressing 1,173 of the 2,465 scheduled cases across 46 active judges. This performance reflects a slight decline in efficiency compared to the previous day, June 22, 2026, when the court achieved a 49.1% hearing rate, disposing of 1,450 out of 2,956 scheduled matters.

Judicial workloads were heavily concentrated, led by Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19, who scheduled 237 cases and heard 96 (40.5%). High-volume efficiency was notable under Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14, who converted 81.3% of his 75-case list (61 heard) alongside a second list of 116 cases (55 heard, 47.4%). Justice Suraj Govindaraj managed a substantial combined docket of 218 cases across Halls 10 and 11, hearing 138 (63.3% average). In Hall 3, Justice Jayant Banerji demonstrated strong throughput by resolving 38 out of 44 scheduled cases on his primary list (86.4%).

Procedural analysis shows that the "Orders" stage remains the largest docket segment, with 422 of the 843 scheduled cases heard (50.1%). "Admissions" saw 49.2% of its 461 cases heard, while "Final Hearings" posted the lowest clearance rate among major stages at 24.5% (109 of 444). By case type, Writ Petitions (WP) constituted the largest docket share with 1,119 cases scheduled, converting 42.5%—a minor decrease from yesterday’s 46.2% rate. Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) recorded a 49.2% hearing rate (158 of 321), while Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) experienced a significant clearance improvement to 52.3% (149 of 285), up from just 35.0% on the prior day.
